summary: Write failing tests in test_recon_panel.py: new TestReconBurstBadgeAria class with 3 tests. (1) test_bursting_badge_has_aria_label — using MOCK_BURST_STATE (agent-1 bursting, agent-2 idle), assert 'aria-label="agent-1 bursting"' in rendered HTML. (2) test_idle_badge_has_aria_label — assert 'aria-label="agent-2 idle"'. (3) test_no_burst_aria_when_empty — with burst_state=[], assert no burst badge aria-labels appear. Reuse _patch_recon_data helper.; Add aria-label to burst state badge spans in recon.html. Line 38 bursting span: add aria-label="{{ agent.agent_id }} bursting". Line 40 idle span: add aria-label="{{ agent.agent_id }} idle".; Write failing tests in test_recon_panel.py: new TestReconVerdictBadgeAria class with 4 tests. (1) test_ok_verdict_has_aria_label — with verdict severity='ok', assert 'aria-label="Verdict: ok"' in HTML. (2) test_minor_verdict_has_aria_label — verdict severity='minor'. (3) test_serious_verdict_has_aria_label — verdict severity='serious'. (4) test_no_verdict_aria_when_none — verdict=None, assert no verdict aria-label. Reuse _patch_recon_data with verdict overrides.; Add aria-label to verdict severity badge spans in recon.html. Lines 82, 84, 86, 88, 90: add aria-label="Verdict: {{ verdict.severity }}" to each severity span. The prefix 'Verdict:' disambiguates since raw text like 'ok' is meaningless without context.; Write failing tests in test_recon_panel.py: new TestReconRunStatusBadgeAria class with 4 tests. (1) test_completed_run_has_aria_label — assert 'aria-label="Run completed"'. (2) test_running_run_has_aria_label — run with status='running'. (3) test_failed_run_has_aria_label — run with status='failed'. (4) test_no_run_aria_when_empty — runs=[], no run status aria-labels. Reuse _patch_recon_data with runs overrides.; Add aria-label to run status badge spans in recon.html. Lines 125, 127, 129, 131, 133, 135: add aria-label="Run {{ run.status }}" to each status span. The 'Run' prefix provides table context.; Write failing tests in test_app.py: new TestAriaLivePolling class with 4 tests. (1) test_memory_section_has_aria_live — fetch '/', locate 'hx-get="/partials/memory"' section, assert 'aria-live="polite"' in surrounding HTML. (2) test_recon_section_has_aria_live — same for /partials/recon. (3) test_orchestrators_section_has_aria_live — same for /partials/orchestrators. (4) test_three_aria_live_regions — assert html.count('aria-live="polite"') == 3. Follow TestHtmxTimeout section-extraction pattern (find hx-get index, extract html[idx-200:idx+500]).; Add aria-live='polite' attribute to each of the three <section> elements in index.html. Line 6 memory section: <section aria-live="polite" hx-get=... >. Line 19 recon section: same. Line 32 orchestrators section: same.

Completed all 8 steps. Added ARIA labels to memory status dots (role='status' + dynamic aria-label), recon badges (burst state, verdict severity, run status), orchestrator badges (running/completed + progress bar), and aria-live='polite' to all three polling sections in index.html. 241 tests passing.

Implemented 4-step is_held plan from task description. step-1 (efdb567): failing tests for ModuleLockTable.is_held(). step-2 (2a5905d): add is_held() method. step-3 (e53c830): failing async tests for acquire_next() duplicate prevention. step-4 (e78028b): guard in acquire_next() — if self.lock_table.is_held(task_id): continue. 41/41 tests pass. Bug fixed. BLOCKER: plan.json on disk was replaced mid-execution with a different 8-step _dispatched set plan. Cannot update plan.json status fields. Escalated as esc-42-3.
